item.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.

item.title: Microsoft patches record number of security vulnerabilities, citing its use of AI
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Microsoft's monthly release of security fixes, dubbed Patch Tuesday, resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across the company's product line, thanks to discoveries with AI.

item.title: Stripe and Advent reportedly offered to buy PayPal for around $53.4B
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have reportedly submitted a joint bid to acquire PayPal in a deal valued at approximately $53.4 billion.  Reuters reports that the offer was submitted earlier this month and is backed by roughly $50 billion in committed bank financing. Under the proposal, Stripe and Advent would jointly own PayPal, […]

item.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba’s Qwen AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple Intelligence is headed to China after regulators approved Apple’s AI services through a partnership with Alibaba. The long-rumored deal will bring Alibaba’s Qwen AI models to Apple’s operating systems, marking a major expansion of the company’s generative AI platform into one of its most important markets.

item.title: Spotify expands parent-managed accounts to users on its free tier
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The feature was previously only available to paid subscribers.

item.title: India bets billions on breaking China’s grip on smartphone manufacturing
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: New Delhi announced a $6.5 billion smartphone manufacturing program and a $13.3 billion semiconductor push to deepen India's electronics supply chain.

item.title: US charges Russian ‘bulletproof’ web hosts over cyberattacks that netted $62M from cybercrime victims
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The 2024 indictment, now unsealed, accuses three Russians and two web hosts of aiding hackers and profiting from cybercrime.

item.title: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not just models
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

item.title: Rime picks up $24M Series A to help enterprises field customer calls
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Rime is handling over 100 million calls each month across multiple companies.

item.title: Reelful’s AI turns your camera roll into short-form videos for social media
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The app is designed for people who want to create social content, but find traditional video editing tools too complex or time-consuming.

item.title: Indian AI coding startup Emergent becomes a unicorn with $130M Series C
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The startup has reached a $120 million annualized revenue run rate and more than 200,000 paying customers.

item.title: Vint Cerf is working on a plan to unleash AI agents on the open internet
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The guy behind TCP/IP is working on a standard for identifying AI agents in the wild.

item.title: Why Realta Fusion is building a fusion reactor at an old hot dog factory
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: An old Oscar Mayer factory in Wisconsin will become America's latest fusion power research and development hub.

item.title: Backed by $60M in funding, Oak steps out of stealth to fix the identity mess that AI agents are making worse
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Co-founded by serial entrepreneur Shai Morag, Israeli identity management startup Oak is emerging out of stealth with $60 million in seed funding.

item.title: A SpaceX vet raised $65M to pull wire harnesses out of the Cold War era
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Someone has to bundle all the wires that go into rockets, missiles, and satellites.

item.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

item.title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.

item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

item.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

item.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.
